stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 4 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 20 observations with mean 51.7 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.8). region B has 20 observations with mean 66.7 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.7). region C has 15 observations with mean 63.1 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.8). region D has 8 observations with mean 64.2 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 7.6). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.9 and 17.2 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region C: 63.0 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 63.1). Range [62.8, 63.1] as between-region SD varies from 6.9 to 17.2. Pooled mean: 60.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 60.8 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 4 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=11.5: B = 0.954.
Interval [62.8, 63.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (63.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (60.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
